remember: Students will be able to recall the major drainage systems of India.
Students will be able to identify the different types of rivers and their characteristics.
Students will be able to name the major rivers of India and their tributaries.
understand: Students will be able to explain the formation of drainage systems.
Students will be able to describe the role of rivers in shaping the landscape.
Students will be able to understand the concept of a watershed and its importance.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of drainage systems to understand the impact of human activities on rivers and watersheds.
Students will be able to use maps and other geographic tools to analyze drainage patterns.
Students will be able to propose measures to conserve and manage river water resources.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors influencing the drainage pattern of a region.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the characteristics of different river systems.
Students will be able to evaluate the economic, social, and environmental significance of rivers and drainage systems.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the major components of the drainage system.
To appreciate the role of rivers in the development of human settlements and civilizations.
To recognize the importance of conservation and management of river water resources.
board_exam_objectives: To test the students' knowledge of the major drainage systems of India.
To assess the students' ability to identify and explain the characteristics of different types of rivers.
To evaluate the students' understanding of the human impact on rivers and watersheds.
